description: click landed on a SELECT element. The error tells us to use type_text. Don't click again.

seed.content:
[Current page context — applies to this user message and supersedes older page context for phrases like "this page". URL: https://example.com/signup — Title: Sign up]

pick "Premium" as the plan

seed.name: click
seed.content: {"success":false,"tag":"SELECT","text":"Free","error":"CANNOT CLICK a <select> dropdown — clicking opens a native OS popup that cannot be controlled. The dropdown is now focused (current: \"Free\"). Use type_text({text: \"option name\"}) to change the value. Available options: Free, Pro, Premium"}

antiPatterns.reason: Will hit the SELECT again — error message explicitly forbids this.
Coord click on a SELECT triggers the native popup we can't control.

expected.successRubric: Use type_text with the option name — the SELECT is already focused per the error. Do not click again.
